# AI / LLM agent ツール（llm-agents.nix由来）
final: prev:
let
  inherit (prev.stdenv.hostPlatform) system;
in
{
  inherit (prev._llm-agents.packages.${system})
    claude-code
    ccusage
    codex
    opencode
    vibe-kanban
    cursor-agent
    entire
    agent-browser
    ;
  cage = prev._cage.packages.${system}.default;
}

file.content:
# home-managerの共通パッケージリスト（Linux/macOS共通）
{ pkgs, lib, ... }:

{
  home.packages =
    (with pkgs.llm-agents; [
      claude-code
      ccusage
      opencode
      rtk
      vibe-kanban
      cursor-agent
      agent-browser
      entire
    ])
    ++ (with pkgs; [
      # Overlay packages
      continues
      # Version Control
      git
      git-wt
      git-lfs
      git-filter-repo
      lazygit
      jujutsu
      jj-desc

      # Development Tools
      repiq
      nil
      nix-init
      ni

      # Google Suite
      gogcli

      # CLI Utilities
      curl
      wget
      aria2
      xh
      ripgrep
      fzf
      peco
      jq
      jnv
      tokei
      cloc
      similarity-ts
      lsd
      btop
      zoxide
      tree
      glow
      gum
      vhs
      yazi
      imagemagick
      ffmpeg
      python313Packages.markitdown
      stable.yt-dlp
      halloy
      obsidian

      # Network Tools
      tunnelto
      speedtest-cli
      bandwhich
      nmap
      dnsutils

      # Misc
      sl
      fastfetch
    ]);
}

content.text:
完了です。変更まとめ:

| ファイル | 変更内容 |
|---|---|
| `flake.nix` | `_llm-agents = llm-agents;` → `llm-agents.overlays.default` を追加（`pkgs.llm-agents.*` が使えるように） |
| `nix/overlays/ai-tools.nix` | llm-agents の inherit を削除、`cage` のみ残す |
| `nix/modules/home/packages.nix` | `with pkgs.llm-agents` で claude-code, ccusage, opencode, rtk, vibe-kanban, cursor-agent, agent-browser, entire を参照 |
| `nix/modules/home/programs/codex.nix` | `pkgs.codex` → `pkgs.llm-agents.codex` |
| `nix/modules/home/programs/claude-code.nix` | `pkgs.rtk` → `pkgs.llm-agents.rtk` |

自前のオーバーレイで `pkgs` トップレベルに展開する間接参照がなくなり、`pkgs.llm-agents.*` で直接参照するすっきりした構造になりました。

1. Remove the overlay `nix/overlays/dev-tools.nix`

But unlike llm-agents, these inputs (nix-steipete-tools, version-lsp, ghostty, repiq) probably don't have `overlays.default`. They just have `packages.${system}.default`.

Wait, the user said "dev-tools.nixも似た感じで" - I think they mean to also move dev-tools overlay packages into a `programs/dev-tools` module, similar to how we structured the AI tools, so we can manage them in one place instead of scattered across multiple files.

The challenge is that these packages are currently referenced throughout the config as `pkgs.ghostty`, `pkgs.version-lsp`, etc. - ghostty appears in linux-specific configs, version-lsp is in neovim, and they're all wired through the overlay system. I need to figure out whether to create dedicated modules for each or consolidate them, and whether these inputs even expose overlays or if I should just pull the packages directly from the flake inputs. about `llm-agents.overlays.default`. The other passthrough overlays were kept.

I think "似た感じで" means: create `programs/dev-tools.nix` that collects the dev-tool packages, remove them from `packages.nix`, and remove the overlay. But the overlay is needed because these packages need to be in `pkgs` scope for modules like `neovim.nix` to reference them.

Hmm, let me reconsider. Maybe the user wants:
1. Keep the overlay for packages that need to be in `pkgs` scope (version-lsp, ghostty)
2. But move the package references into a `programs/dev-tools.nix` module

Actually I think the simplest interpretation is: make a `programs/dev-tools.nix` that lists the dev-tool packages from the overlays, similar to how `programs/ai-tools.nix` lists AI tool packages. Remove them from `packages.nix`.

But the problem is that some of these packages need to stay in the overlay because they're referenced as `pkgs.xxx` in other modules (version-lsp in neovim.nix, ghostty in linux configs). The overlays still need to exist because `version-lsp` needs `doCheck = false` override and `ghostty` needs to be resolved. to how llm-agents was handled, but adapted for dev-tools since it doesn't have an overlay structure in the flake inputs—I'll just create the programs module and reference the packages directly.
